The GOP is going all-out to grab the momentum.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a5a5/c1f7015/6a850c32/11419023/3696811750/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-august?refcode=rtw_20180805_em-full") Team, our primary election is in just 9 days and right now we are falling behind our grassroots goals. **We have to close this budget gap or we will be forced to cut back on our organizers, get-out-the-vote efforts, and ads -- and we cannot afford to do that.[Please chip in whatever you can here. Every dollar will matter with our election this close.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a5a5/c1f7015/6a850c32/11419023/3696811750/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-august?refcode=rtw_20180805_em-full")** National Republicans and special interests know how important this election is to taking back the Senate, and they are going all-out to ensure they have the momentum going into November. Peter,

This is a big week for us, with events across the District with exciting Democratic candidates, building the wave here in Texas.

[_**Will you pitch in $20 today to power our campaign**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1828672/9640188/-2058851995?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmMzNDNkYi04Nzk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l5iJIRHtn08ucs2hdoqOJgt9PtvKnIHsJ6ah1CyDv14=)? Here's our week ahead:
* **Today,[**I'll join Te** xas Agriculture Commissioner nominee Kim Olson](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1828673/9640189/-1805857011?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmMzNDNkYi04Nzk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l5iJIRHtn08ucs2hdoqOJgt9PtvKnIHsJ6ah1CyDv14=)** in speaking to voters in Northwest Travis County. Col. Olson is a wonderful advocate for Texas farmers, including those in my District who are suffering under the pointless Republican Trade War.

* **Tomorrow,[our campaign is hosting a town hall on flood control in Katy, "Action Before the Storm."](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1828674/9640190/1649910534?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmMzNDNkYi04Nzk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l5iJIRHtn08ucs2hdoqOJgt9PtvKnIHsJ6ah1CyDv14=)** We'll be joined by Texas Governor nominee Lupe Valdez, Congressional nominee Sylvia Garcia (TX-29), and other Democratic leaders in proposing comprehensive solutions to flood control in our region.

* **Thursday,[I'll join U.S. Senate nominee Beto O'Rourke at his town hall in Katy](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1828675/9640191/-1415207633?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmMzNDNkYi04Nzk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l5iJIRHtn08ucs2hdoqOJgt9PtvKnIHsJ6ah1CyDv14=)**. This should be a big one! Beto's campaign has energized all of us who are running in Texas. After this past weekend, when I spent hours block-walking in Katy and speaking to a diverse community of Democratic voters, I'm excited to see the turnout for this event.

* **Saturday** , **I'll be speaking at[the Bastrop County Democratic Party coordinated campaign kickoff event](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1828676/9640192/1975275686?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmMzNDNkYi04Nzk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l5iJIRHtn08ucs2hdoqOJgt9PtvKnIHsJ6ah1CyDv14=) **along with State Sen. Kirk Watson, Congressional nominee Eric Holguin (TX-27) and other Democratic leaders.

Texas Democrats are fired up and ready to go! [**Please continue to support our efforts**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1828677/9640193/-2058851995?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmMzNDNkYi04Nzk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l5iJIRHtn08ucs2hdoqOJgt9PtvKnIHsJ6ah1CyDv14=) as we work with candidates up and down the ticket to win Democratic representation for our communities.

Onward to victory!

Mike Siegel

body:
#
Peter --
Last week, I won the Democratic nomination to be the next United States Senator from Tennessee.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1829460/9644252/-1321470857?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODYxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTNiZGQzNy05MDk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kHsOsR7rEEL2P9f3Wzmq2bguJACkEadD2L0rM0LBMXQ=&refcode=em180806-post-primary-full&amounts=5,15,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=Peter&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Lebanon&state=TN&zip=37087&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10)
This is not where I thought I’d be after I left the Governor’s office. When Andrea and I started talking about it last October, it was a tough decision. But we decided there was still some unfinished business.
**I love my country. I believe in it.**
I believe in the notion of a deliberative process in Washington where our leaders talk about the problems facing our communities, decide how to fix them, and move forward. But somehow, over the last 10 or 15 years, we’ve lost that.
We’ve turned into a country where everybody just stands on opposite sides of the room, shouting at each other. I’d like to be part of the fix for that, to start bringing us back together to find some solutions and get things done.
[**And I’d like your help. Can you add a donation of $10 or whatever you can to join our campaign to fix Washington?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1829461/9644253/-1321470857?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODYxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTNiZGQzNy05MDk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kHsOsR7rEEL2P9f3Wzmq2bguJACkEadD2L0rM0LBMXQ=&refcode=em180806-post-primary-full&amounts=5,15,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=Peter&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Lebanon&state=TN&zip=37087&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10)
**[Donate today](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1829462/9644254/-1321470857?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODYxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTNiZGQzNy05MDk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kHsOsR7rEEL2P9f3Wzmq2bguJACkEadD2L0rM0LBMXQ=&refcode=em180806-post-primary-full&amounts=5,15,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=Peter&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Lebanon&state=TN&zip=37087&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10)**
Since this campaign began, I’ve been traveling all across the state and meeting with an awful lot of people. **They all say the same thing: let’s get things done.** That’s very much the way I tried to operate as governor and very much what I want to bring to Washington as your next Senator.
Tennessee has been a place of enormous opportunity for Andrea and me, and the possibility of representing our state is a real honor. We arrived here when we had been married just 10 months — she had a job, I did not, and we didn’t know anybody in the state.
Over the next 30 years, we were able to build a successful public company together. I became Mayor of Nashville and then Governor of Tennessee, and that didn’t happen without enormous numbers of people stepping up and reaching out to help.
I want to make sure that everybody has these same kinds of opportunities and that same sort of opening, and the federal government has a lot to do with making that happen.
[**Your support is going to be key to getting that message in front of voters in the coming months. Add $10 to our campaign today, and we’ll have the resources we need to keep this race focused on getting things done for people, not partisan politics.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1829463/9644255/-1321470857?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODYxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTNiZGQzNy05MDk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kHsOsR7rEEL2P9f3Wzmq2bguJACkEadD2L0rM0LBMXQ=&refcode=em180806-post-primary-full&amounts=5,15,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=Peter&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Lebanon&state=TN&zip=37087&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10)
**[Donate today](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1829464/9644256/-1321470857?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODYxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMTNiZGQzNy05MDk5LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kHsOsR7rEEL2P9f3Wzmq2bguJACkEadD2L0rM0LBMXQ=&refcode=em180806-post-primary-full&amounts=5,15,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=Peter&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Lebanon&state=TN&zip=37087&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10)**
I grew up in a single-parent household, and my mother made a lot of sacrifices for me. I remember a time when I was in college, she did something for me and I said “Thanks, Mom, and I’ll pay you back someday.”
Then she said “No, I don’t want you to pay me back. I want you to pay me forward. Someday, you’re going to have a wife and some children, I hope, and what I want you to do is put the same commitment into your relationship with them that I have to you.”
So I just want to tell you this — if you’re expecting me to pay you back for all the help you’ve been putting forward, you’re probably going to be disappointed.
**Because I’m going to pay you forward by being the best damn Senator you ever sent to Washington.**
Thanks for being on my team,

clean_body: Peter -- Last week, I won the Democratic nomination to be the next United States Senator from Tennessee. This is not where I thought I’d be after I left the Governor’s office. When Andrea and I started talking about it last October, it was a tough decision. But we decided there was still some unfinished business. I love my country. I believe in it. I believe in the notion of a deliberative process in Washington where our leaders talk about the problems facing our communities, decide how to fix them, and move forward. But somehow, over the last 10 or 15 years, we’ve lost that. We’ve turned into a country where everybody just stands on opposite sides of the room, shouting at each other. I’d like to be part of the fix for that, to start bringing us back together to find some solutions and get things done. Since this campaign began, I’ve been traveling all across the state and meeting with an awful lot of people. They all say the same thing: let’s get things done. That’s very much the way I tried to operate as governor and very much what I want to bring to Washington as your next Senator. Tennessee has been a place of enormous opportunity for Andrea and me, and the possibility of representing our state is a real honor. We arrived here when we had been married just 10 months — she had a job, I did not, and we didn’t know anybody in the state. Over the next 30 years, we were able to build a successful public company together. I became Mayor of Nashville and then Governor of Tennessee, and that didn’t happen without enormous numbers of people stepping up and reaching out to help. I want to make sure that everybody has these same kinds of opportunities and that same sort of opening, and the federal government has a lot to do with making that happen. I grew up in a single-parent household, and my mother made a lot of sacrifices for me. I remember a time when I was in college, she did something for me and I said “Thanks, Mom, and I’ll pay you back someday.” Then she said “No, I don’t want you to pay me back. I want you to pay me forward. Someday, you’re going to have a wife and some children, I hope, and what I want you to do is put the same commitment into your relationship with them that I have to you.” So I just want to tell you this — if you’re expecting me to pay you back for all the help you’ve been putting forward, you’re probably going to be disappointed. Because I’m going to pay you forward by being the best damn Senator you ever sent to Washington. Thanks for being on my team, — Phil Bredesen PAID FOR BY BREDESEN FOR SENATE P.O. Box 92010 Nashville, TN 37209

The White House • August 6, 2018
# This morning’s announcement on Iran
**In May, President Donald J. Trump** withdrew America from the disastrous Iran nuclear deal. “Our policy is based on a clear-eyed assessment of the Iranian dictatorship, its sponsorship of terrorism, and its continuing aggression in the Middle East and all around the world,” the President said. **In making that announcement,** President Trump laid out two “wind-down periods” of 90 days and 180 days for business activities in or involving Iran. Today marks the final day of that initial wind-down period. Tomorrow, August 7, [_sanctions will be reimposed_](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA2LjkzMzc3MzcxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwNi45MzM3NzM3MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY1NjI4JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&100&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/statement-president-reimposition-united-states-sanctions-respect-iran/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=wh?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) on the purchase of U.S. bank notes by the Government of Iran, Iran’s trade in gold and other precious metals, and a range of other financial and industrial transactions. **Remaining sanctions will be reimposed** on November 5. **The Iranian regime has exploited** the global financial system to fund its malign activities, including supporting terrorism, promoting ruthless regimes, and abusing the human rights of its own people. Since the President announced his decision to withdraw from the deal, the Administration has sanctioned 38 Iran-related targets. [_Learn more about this morning’s Executive Order on Iran._](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA2LjkzMzc3MzcxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwNi45MzM3NzM3MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY1NjI4JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&101&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/president-donald-j-trump-reimposing-sanctions-lifted-horrible-iran-deal/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) **Background:** [ _The Iran deal was defective. A new one will require real commitments._](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA2LjkzMzc3MzcxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwNi45MzM3NzM3MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY1NjI4JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&102&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/articles/president-trump-says-iran-deal-defective-core-new-one-will-require-real-commitments/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)
## The opioid crisis: Telling your story
**Earlier this year,** President Trump laid out a bold vision for stopping America’s opioid epidemic. Since then, the entire Administration has mobilized to combat the crisis. **The first step** of the President’s plan is to address the driving forces behind the epidemic. That starts with reducing excess demand for drugs—including legal medications that can be misused—through education, awareness, and prevention of over-prescription. **To help achieve this goal** , President Trump is asking all Americans to share personal stories of how opioid abuse and misuse has affected their lives. Visit [_crisisnextdoor.gov_](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA2LjkzMzc3MzcxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwNi45MzM3NzM3MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY1NjI4JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&103&&&https://www.crisisnextdoor.gov/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) to share your experience or that of a loved one, and watch the moving stories of those whose lives have changed because of this deadly epidemic. [_The crisis next door: A message from President Trump_](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA2LjkzMzc3MzcxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwNi45MzM3NzM3MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY1NjI4JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&104&&&https://www.crisisnextdoor.gov/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) **The plan:** [_How we will win the war on opioids_](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA2LjkzMzc3MzcxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwNi45MzM3NzM3MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY1NjI4JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&105&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/articles/will-win-war-opioids/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)

| Hey Friend, Wow. We are only one day away from the election. We have come so far, and I am incredibly proud of our campaign and all of our supporters. This would simply be impossible without your help. Both Austin and I feel very good about tomorrow and I believe that we are going to win. But there’s still work to be done! Thank you so much, Friend. Please check out the campaign update below: **Judge Napolitano Comments On The Race:** Austin’s former boss, Judge Andrew Napolitano, decided to comment on the U.S. Senate race in Missouri. As you know, Austin and Napolitano are good friends and he had very kind things to say about Austin. You can read his statement below: **Twitter Ban:** Last week Austin caused an unintended media storm by getting a 12 hour ban on Twitter. Why? Well, that’s where it gets weird. Austin received the ban after posting a GIF from Twitter’s own library in response to a leftist journalist accusing him of assisting Russian hackers. The hackers (supposedly) targeted Austin’s opponent, Senator Claire McCaskill. The journalist made this accusation because our campaign accepts bitcoin. Folks, I can’t make this stuff up. It was ridiculous. You can read about the story in the [**Daily Caller by clicking here.**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fdailycaller.com%2F2018%2F07%2F31%2Faustin-petersen-twitter-ban%2F&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=1) This is not the first time social media giants have censored conservatives. Facebook has also banned Austin previously for giving away an AR-15. **New Radio Ad:** Have you heard our new radio ad that we are blasting through Missouri? [**You can listen to it by clicking here.**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2FAP4Liberty%2Fvideos%2F1923972650995039%2F&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=2) We are placing radio buys in key areas of the state where we believe that we can turn out additional Austin Petersen voters. **Pre Election Media Coverage:** Austin has been all over the news in the past week. I can’t even keep track of it all. Our campaign is generating so much media attention at the perfect time. You can read an interview with Austin in the Columbia Tribune by [**clicking here.**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.columbiatribune.com%2Fnews%2F20180805%2Fmeet-candidates-for-state-senator&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=3) The Missourian profiled Austin before the election [**here**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.columbiamissourian.com%2Fnews%2Flocal%2Fwhat-you-need-to-know-about-tuesday-s-primary-election%2Farticle_48547620-9430-11e8-9cd0-677e71c69f1a.html%3Futm_medium%3Dsocial%26utm_source%3Dfacebook%26utm_campaign%3Duser-share&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=4)**.** And you can read more media coverage of the campaign by clicking [**here**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fnewstalkkzrg.com%2F2018%2F07%2F31%2Fparson-hawley-petersen-speak-in-neosho-on-historical-mo-senate-race%2F&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=5)**,**[**here**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwestnewsmagazine.com%2F2018%2F08%2F03%2F98298%2F2018-election-preview-candidates-and-issues-on-the-ballot&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=6) , and [**here!**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tonyskansascity.com%2F2018%2F08%2Fupstart-missouri-senate-candidate.html&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=7) As you can see Austin’s message of limited government and liberty is getting across in the media. **Ghost Gunner Giveaway:** I know a lot of you are wondering when we are going to announce the winners for the giveaway! Austin will announce the winners tomorrow morning in an email. So make sure you check your inbox! However, not everyone is so excited about the giveaway. Austin even received a death threat for giving away one of these machines. So much for the tolerant left, huh? [**You can read that story by clicking here.**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ozarksfirst.com%2Fnews%2Fmissouri-candidate-receives-death-threats-for-gun-device-giveaway%2F1336086201&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=8) [**If you have not entered the giveaway yet, make sure you click here and add your name >>>**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/ghost_gunner?e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=9) **Volunteers Needed:** [**Just one more day until the election! Please make phone calls! You can volunteer by clicking here >>>**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/volunteer?e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_update&n=10) If you are already making calls, please commit to making calls both today and tomorrow. We need your help! This is one of the most effective ways to help Austin win. Let’s reach as many voters as possible before polls close tomorrow. Thank you so much, Friend. Jeffrey Carson
Campaign Manager
Austin Petersen for U.S. Senate

Thank you so much, Friend. Please check out the campaign update below: Judge Napolitano Comments On The Race: Austin’s former boss, Judge Andrew Napolitano, decided to comment on the U.S. Senate race in Missouri. As you know, Austin and Napolitano are good friends and he had very kind things to say about Austin. You can read his statement below: Twitter Ban: Last week Austin caused an unintended media storm by getting a 12 hour ban on Twitter. Why? Well, that’s where it gets weird. Austin received the ban after posting a GIF from Twitter’s own library in response to a leftist journalist accusing him of assisting Russian hackers. The hackers (supposedly) targeted Austin’s opponent, Senator Claire McCaskill. The journalist made this accusation because our campaign accepts bitcoin. Folks, I can’t make this stuff up. It was ridiculous. You can read about the story in the This is not the first time social media giants have censored conservatives. Facebook has also banned Austin previously for giving away an AR-15. New Radio Ad: Have you heard our new radio ad that we are blasting through Missouri? We are placing radio buys in key areas of the state where we believe that we can turn out additional Austin Petersen voters. Pre Election Media Coverage: Austin has been all over the news in the past week. I can’t even keep track of it all. Our campaign is generating so much media attention at the perfect time. You can read an interview with Austin in the Columbia Tribune by The Missourian profiled Austin before the election . And you can read more media coverage of the campaign by clicking , and As you can see Austin’s message of limited government and liberty is getting across in the media. Ghost Gunner Giveaway: I know a lot of you are wondering when we are going to announce the winners for the giveaway! Austin will announce the winners tomorrow morning in an email. So make sure you check your inbox! However, not everyone is so excited about the giveaway. Austin even received a death threat for giving away one of these machines. So much for the tolerant left, huh? Volunteers Needed: If you are already making calls, please commit to making calls both today and tomorrow. We need your help! This is one of the most effective ways to help Austin win. Let’s reach as many voters as possible before polls close tomorrow. Thank you so much, Friend. Jeffrey Carson Campaign Manager Austin Petersen for U.S. Senate Paid for by Austin Petersen for US Senate ()

body:
We can't ignore the Hogan fundraising machine.

| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a8f8/7ab0e2c4/7fcbf8cf/349b2dcf/3837143539/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-3-months?refcode=OHQ08068A") Friend, Three months from today, Maryland voters will be heading to the polls. That's why I wanted to share some quick numbers with you, because there's no time to waste:
* Earlier this summer, Larry Hogan invested $1.3 million on TV ads in the Baltimore and Washington-area media markets
* As of his last campaign finance filing, he had nearly $9 million on hand
* No one's run a poll since Ben won the primary -- but everyone's predicting long-shot odds

**We knew this fight would be a tough one, but with only three months left until Election Day, we're starting to feel the heat (no pun intended).** [**If you want to win, it's time to step up: Donate $5 or more today to turn Maryland blue this November.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a8f8/7ab0e2c4/7fcbf8cf/349b2dcf/3837143539/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-3-months?refcode=OHQ08068A") _Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately:_ | | [**$5 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a8f8/7ab0e2c4/7fcbf8cf/349b2dce/3837143539/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-3-months?refcode=OHQ08068A&amount=5&express_lane=true")

As the Finance Director for this campaign, I can't ignore Larry Hogan's fundraising machine.
He's raking in huge donations, but from exactly the wrong people: relatives of Chris Christie, big-time developers, pharmaceutical companies, and more.
That's not who we are, and that's not what this campaign is about. It's built by you, for you -- by real grassroots donors giving an average online donation of $34. That's because we know if we build something together, we can build it better, and we can build it stronger.
Right-wing partisans and corporate donors need not apply. This is about bringing everyone to the table -- and it's why I know Ben is the right choice for Maryland.
[**We have just three months left to prove it, so I hope you'll join now: Donate $5 or more to fund the fight to bring change to Maryland this November.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a8f8/7ab0e2c4/7fcbf8cf/349b2dcf/3837143539/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-3-months?refcode=OHQ08068A")
Thanks,
Erica

body:
**Bob Hugin "slashed price for cancer drug in Russia while raising it here."**
_NJ.com, 08/05/18_

[**While Bob Hugin and Donald Trump kowtow to the Kremlin, Democratic Senator Bob Menendez is fighting to lower the costs of prescription drugs and give more New Jerseyans access to health care. If you want a Senator who will fight for New Jersey, not Russia, chip in $3 or more today.**](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/c846ca1/3af3c5d/3bfc4d30/4ec3990e/131466091/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/menendez_russiapricing?gpsid=5_73_2608&refcode=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bmene&utm_content=1+-+While+Bob+Hugin+and+Donald+Trump+kowtow+") **[DONATE](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/c846ca1/3af3c5d/3bfc4d30/4ec39901/131466091/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/menendez_russiapricing?gpsid=5_73_2608&refcode=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bmene&utm_content=2+-+DONATE")** Friend,

With Bob Hugin, the more we learn, the worse it gets.

By now we have all heard that Bob Hugin made hundreds of millions price-gouging cancer patients over the years. Now _NJ.com_ is reporting that Bob Hugin raised prices on American cancer patients while giving huge discounts to Russia.

It's a form of treason against American cancer patients.

According to the report, a 21-tablet supply of Revlimid was $14,529 in the United States while the same product cost only $4,175 in Russia after Hugin gave into Putin's demands for a price break. That means Bob Hugin charged Americans a whopping 248 percent more than Russians for the same medicine.

[**New Jersey needs a U.S. Senator who will fight to lower the costs of prescription drugs for American taxpayers -- not rip them off for huge profits. Chip in $3 or more and help make sure Democratic Senator Bob Menendez beats Republican Bob Hugin in November.**](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/c846ca1/3af3c5d/3bfc4d30/4ec39900/131466091/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/menendez_russiapricing?gpsid=5_73_2608&refcode=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bmene&utm_content=3+-+New+Jersey+needs+a+US+Senator+who+will+f") **[Donate](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/c846ca1/3af3c5d/3bfc4d30/4ec39903/131466091/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/menendez_russiapricing?gpsid=5_73_2608&refcode=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bmene&utm_content=4+-+Donate")** There is no question that Senator Bob Menendez has and will continue to put New Jersey's families first.

While Senator Menendez fought to give over one million New Jerseyans access to affordable health care and to stop Bob Hugin's friends from repealing Obamacare, Hugin spent millions on lobbyists to defeat legislation in Congress that would lower drug prices for Americans.

And after 2016, Senator Menendez worked to pass crippling sanctions in response to Russian interference in our elections and their continued aggression towards our allies.

Hugin and Trump may cave to Russian pressure, but Senator Bob Menendez does not.

New Jerseyans deserve a leader in the U.S. Senate who will stand up to greedy drug CEOs like Bob Hugin -- not one who will work to further the interests of big pharmaceutical companies, Donald Trump, and the Kremlin.

[**Chip in $3 or more and help Democratic Senator Bob Menendez win re-election this November. It has never mattered more.**](https://go.menendezfornj.com/page/m/c846ca1/3af3c5d/3bfc4d30/4ec39902/131466091/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/menendez_russiapricing?gpsid=5_73_2608&refcode=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180806_Russia_fnd_gq_cust__MS_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bmene&utm_content=5+-+Chip+in+3+or+more+and+help+Democratic+Se")

Thank you,

Michael

Michael Soliman
Campaign Chair
Menendez for Senate

| | | **Indiana Farm Bureau ELECT PAC Endorses Congressman Jim Banks for Re-Election to Congress**
**Fort Wayne, IN.** \- Today, Congressman Jim Banks released this statement regarding the decision of Indiana Farm Bureau ELECT PAC to endorse his re-election to Congress:

_"I’m very proud to receive the endorsement of the Farm Bureau ELECT PAC representing farmers across the Third District. Agriculture is very important to our district and I promise to continue to be a leader and voice for our district’s farmers in my second term.__”_

Indiana Farm Bureau ELECT PAC is the nonpartisan political action committee associated with Indiana Farm Bureau. ELECT PAC endorsements are made through a multi-step process, starting with recommendations from Farm Bureau members who live in the district.

To learn more about Jim Banks’ conservative record, please visit his website at [www.JimBanks.us](https://jimbanks.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9f5396c0af44767a2db1e30c&id=1962f4435f&e=bfe3be83a5).

We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c4fcd/24c69fd9/264e08843/1884e70c/4050977260/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFET13N_digital_email_20180806_TrumpMoney_inact&source=WQEFET13N_digital_email_20180806_TrumpMoney_inact")
---

Peter,

President Trump isn't leaving the midterms up to chance.

In fact, he contributed to a slew of Republican congressional campaigns, including 33 candidates who are running against our women.

And his campaign has given the maximum donation -- $2,000 per candidate -- to 100 Republicans running this year.

He's buying Republicans' loyalty, so they, as Trump's campaign manager said, "can maintain the momentum of the Trump agenda."

It's also a means of keeping our women out of office where he _knows_ they will fight back against that agenda.

**[Donate $3 to help pro-choice Democratic women defeat Trump-supported Republicans in November.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c4fcd/24c69fd9/264e08843/1884e70d/4050977260/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180806_email_TrumpMoney?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+prochoice+Democratic+wo&utm_campaign=WQEFET13N_digital_email_20180806_TrumpMoney_inact&source=WQEFET13N_digital_email_20180806_TrumpMoney_inact")**

Of those 33 Republicans, three are running against our Senate women -- Claire McCaskill (MO), Jacky Rosen (NV), and Tina Smith (MN) -- 29 are running to hold on to red House seats we know our women can turn blue, and one is running to flip an open blue seat.

And some of these Republicans -- including Claire's likely opponent Josh Hawley and Sharice Davids' (KS-03) opponent Kevin Yoder -- have received an even more blatant stamp of Trump's approval: his endorsement.

Why? Because Trump is scared.

He knows Democrats, especially our pro-choice Democratic women, have what it takes to flip Congress this fall.

**Donate $3 to help pro-choice Democratic women flip the House and Senate:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Defeat-Trump-Supported-GOP](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c4fcd/24c69fd9/264e08843/1884e70e/4050977260/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180806_email_TrumpMoney?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgDefeatTrumpSuppo&utm_campaign=WQEFET13N_digital_email_20180806_TrumpMoney_inact&source=WQEFET13N_digital_email_20180806_TrumpMoney_inact")**

Thanks,

Christina Reynolds
Vice President of Communications, EMILY's List

name: Steve Sisolak
subject: Voting rights *still* under attack
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY FRIENDS FOR STEVE SISOLAK
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/NAkNdZtFITHKfkwsbzxPvjkIbQ_5-G0vFAYWs5Ha1nA=/6GRNEgwuHvIfdO9ocrM6d4qQSqVeT0rX5O0q36qwOro=)
---
Dear Friend,

Voting is our fundamental right as Americans. But across our history, it's a fundamental right routinely under attack.

On this day in 1965, the Voting Rights Act became law. It was a landmark bill -- it guaranteed voting protections for African Americans after nearly a century of systematic second-class citizenship and racially-motivated policy decisions.

More than 50 years later, voting rights are STILL under attack. On the anniversary of this crucial act of freedom, it's more important than ever that we protect the ballot box for all Nevadans and all Americans.

**[The only way to honor the legacy of the Voting Rights Act is to USE YOUR VOTE. Click here to say you'll make your voice heard in November >>](https://act.myngp.com/el/NAkNdZtFITHKfkwsbzxPvjkIbQ_5-G0vFAYWs5Ha1nA=/nDHjSBpK2TmJ6spVAw2rVK3wHMIQVCtAtJ1z1djSHas=?gpsid=37_57_2638&ms=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&refcode=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ssiso)**
[I'M Voting](https://act.myngp.com/el/NAkNdZtFITHKfkwsbzxPvjkIbQ_5-G0vFAYWs5Ha1nA=/nDHjSBpK2TmJ6spVAw2rVK3wHMIQVCtAtJ1z1djSHas=?gpsid=37_57_2638&ms=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&refcode=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ssiso)
As our governor, I'll be committed to ensuring every law-abiding Nevadan is eligible to vote. But we can only do that if YOU speak up this November. If we're not voting, we're not winning. And if Adam Laxalt makes it into the governor's office, I fear our that fundamental right will be in peril.

**[So make yourself heard now. Add your name, join your neighbors, and promise you'll be voting with us this November.](https://act.myngp.com/el/NAkNdZtFITHKfkwsbzxPvjkIbQ_5-G0vFAYWs5Ha1nA=/nDHjSBpK2TmJ6spVAw2rVK3wHMIQVCtAtJ1z1djSHas=?gpsid=37_57_2638&ms=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&refcode=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180806_vravot_fld_gq_act__SS_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ssiso)**

It's too important to stay silent. Use your vote to protect your vote.

Thank you,

Steve Sisolak

subject: URGENT: 4 ways to help Austin
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Austin Petersen for US Senate
body:
| Hello Friend,[****](https://www.austinpetersen.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.anedot.com%2Faustinpetersen%2Fdonate_tb_today%3Futm_campaign%3D090618_vol%26source_code%3Demail%26email%3Ddpwillis67%40gmail.com%26first_name%3D%26last_name%3D%26street%3D%26zip%3D%26city%3D%26state%3D%26employer%3D%26occupation%3D%26phone%3D%26amount%3D1000&e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_vol&n=1) Tomorrow is the big day. We have less than 24 hours left to do everything in our power to elect Austin as the Republican nominee for U.S. Senate. Personally, I can’t wait to see expression of Josh Hawley’s face when we crush him! We can’t take anything for granted, though. We’ve got work to do. Here are FOUR things you can do that don’t require donating to help elect Austin Petersen to the U.S. Senate: **Text, email, and call your friends:** If you live in Missouri or have friends that live there, it is your #1 priority to get them to show up and vote for Austin Petersen tomorrow! I cannot stress this enough. Friends and family members trust people they know when it comes to who they are voting for, so please text, email, and call your friends and family. When you leave the voting booth, text them a photo of you with your “I voted” sticker and ask them to do the same! Your personal endorsement and encouragement is imperative to get them out to the polls tomorrow! So please personally reach out to everyone in Missouri that you know and ask them to request a Republican ballot and vote for Austin Petersen! **Make phone calls:** If you are already making phone calls for the campaign, please crank out more! If you can make phone calls during the day today and tomorrow that would be great. [**You can make calls by clicking here >>>**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/volunteer?e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_vol&n=2). It’s easy, completely free, and can be done anywhere that you have an internet connection - even from your phone! We will be making calls until the polls close tomorrow evening, and we need as many ninjas as possible to do it. Even if you’ve never made calls before, it only takes a few minutes to get setup, so please volunteer if you’re able. **Call local radio stations:** Calling into your local radio stations and encouraging people to vote for Austin is an awesome way to get our message on the air for free. Think about how many people you can reach. Please call into Missouri radio stations and talk about Austin’s campaign. [**I’ve compiled all of their contact information on the website. Click here to find your local radio station to call >>>**](https://www.austinpetersen.com/radio_station_info?e=ccccbfabdb12429692987ee06467ce16&utm_source=austinpetersen&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=090618_vol&n=3) **Social media:** Please post on social media why you are supporting Austin Petersen for Senate. If you have Twitter, please make sure you use the hashtag, #MOsen. Ask your friends to like, share, and retweet your posts so they reach as many people as they can. You can also share Austin’s posts on Facebook and retweet him on Twitter. Thank you so much, Friend. We are counting on your help! Heather Coil
Communications Director
Austin Petersen for U.S. Senate

If we don’t win, we won’t have the chance to defeat Senator Baldwin in November. So friend, we HAVE to work together now to make sure we succeed. Leah \-----forwarded message below----- Friend, In less than 2 weeks, we’ll reach the biggest milestone of our campaign: the Republican primary. And I'll be honest with you, friend. I'm worried... After winning the endorsement of the grassroots at the Wisconsin GOP state convention, the National Rifle Association, and support from voters all over our great state, DC special interests are set on trashing my record with personal, baseless attacks. Senator Baldwin herself is already spending out of her own campaign coffers to attack me — showing how desperate she is. These smear tactics have no place in Wisconsin and will only ensure that we re-elect ultraliberal Tammy Baldwin who supports obstruction and the status quo. That's why I have to count on you so much right now to make sure we pull off a victory on August 14. Friend, I know we have what it takes to bring the Wisconsin Way to Washington. I know we are strong enough to change DC politics as usual. And friend, I know we will be motivated, smart, and bold enough to finally fire Senator Baldwin in November. But first, we have to win this primary. We desperately need a proven conservative to stand with President Trump to build the wall, defend the unborn and protect our Constitution. Now that we’re in the homestretch, your support matters more than ever. So what do you say, friend: Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

name: Anniversary - Kaine Petitions Team
subject: Tell Congress to restore the Voting Rights Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
53 years ago today, the Voting Rights Act of 1965 was signed into law, prohibiting racial discrimination in voting. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek,

**[53 years ago today, the Voting Rights Act of 1965 was signed into law, prohibiting racial discrimination in access to voting.](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12ff/3e0906/6f946c97/3e3502af/2953419218/VEsH/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/protect-voting-rights?source=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+53+years+ago+today+the+Voting+Rights+Act&utm_campaign=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition")**

But five years ago, the Supreme Court gutted that landmark legislation, opening the door to allow states and local governments to pass laws undermining the constitutional right of every American to participate in elections.

At this crucial moment in our nation's history, we should be making it easier to vote, not harder. Tim is committed to fully restoring the Voting Rights Act.

[If you are, too, sign your name and tell Congress that it's time to restore the Voting Rights Act >>](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12ff/3e0906/6f946c97/3e3502ae/2953419218/VEsE/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/protect-voting-rights?source=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+If+you+are+too+sign+your+name+and+tell+C&utm_campaign=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition")

Here in Virginia, we've worked hard to expand access to the ballot box. But many communities across the country have moved in the opposite direction. Republican lawmakers have taken advantage of the Supreme Court's decision to strike down key parts of the Voting Rights Act by passing laws that make voting more difficult than it ever should be. This is unacceptable.

**This year 's upcoming midterm elections will decide some of the most important issues our nation has faced in a generation.** There is too much on the line for us to sit and let harsh voting laws prevent Americans from having their voices heard.

[Sign on and tell Congress to pass legislation restoring the Voting Rights Act now.](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12ff/3e0906/6f946c97/3e3502ad/2953419218/VEsF/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/protect-voting-rights?source=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=3+-+Sign+on+and+tell+Congress+to+pass+legisl&utm_campaign=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition")

This issue is bigger than any one campaign or election: **It 's about protecting one of the most fundamental rights of our nation -- a right that generations of people fought and laid down their lives for, and that future generations may not have access to if we don't stand up now.**

Tim has sponsored legislation to give citizens the protections they need to exercise their constitutional right to vote in the past, and continues that fight today. **Tell Congress you stand with him by adding your name today:** | [**ADD MY NAME**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12ff/3e0906/6f946c97/3e3502ac/2953419218/VEsC/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/protect-voting-rights?source=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=4+-+ADD+MY+NAME&utm_campaign=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition")
---
Thanks,

_Team Kaine_

[https://secure.timkaine.com/Restore-the-VRA](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12ff/3e0906/6f946c97/3e3502a3/2953419218/VEsD/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/protect-voting-rights?source=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=5+-+httpssecuretimkainecomRestoretheVRA&utm_campaign=20180806_1_PET_TEAM_VRA-Petition")

name: Team Scholten
subject: A Recap: NetRoots in New Orleans in Five Pictures
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Peter,

J.D. and a couple members of Team Scholten joined 3,000 activists over the last few days for NetRoots Nation, an annual gathering of progressives that this year was held in New Orleans. We wanted to share a couple images from the trip that demonstrate why we’re all working so hard to get J.D. elected.

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP01/1/61580/images/NetRoots%2018%202%20Warren.png]

Here’s J.D. backstage with Senator Elizabeth Warren. Steve King isn’t just Iowa’s problem, he’s bad for the whole country. We’re creating a national movement to defeat him.

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP01/1/61580/images/NetRoots%2018%204%20Pakman.png]

Here’s J.D. doing an interview with David Pakman of Free Speech TV. J.D. is fighting to bring attention to the people of this district and the problems that we’re facing.

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP01/1/61580/images/NetRoots%2018%207%20Brazile.png]

Here’s J.D. with former DNC Chair Donna Brazile. After hearing J.D. was running against the divisive Steve King, she extended her arm up for a high-five and said, “What can I do to help?”

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP01/1/61580/images/NetRoots%2018%203%20Panel.png]

Here’s J.D. at a panel discussion. Unlike Steve King who won’t do Town Hall meetings or meet with voters regularly, J.D. is out on the trail every day talking with voters and listening to their concerns .

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP01/1/61580/images/NetRoots%2018%208%20crowd.png]

Power of the people. One of the things we’re most proud of on this campaign is that it’s people-powered. We’ve outraised Steve King 2-to-1 over the last 3 quarters despite J.D. refusing to take corporate PAC money. We’ve done it on the strength of over 12,000 individual donors just like YOU! Help us show Steve King what the power of this movement can do with a contribution today! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/picturerecap]

Standing Tall for All,
Team Scholten

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/picturerecap [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/picturerecap]

Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee

Scholten4Iowa
P.O. Box 3531
Sioux City, IA 51102
United States

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: My parents fought for this right
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
Today marks the 53rd anniversary of the passage of the Voting Rights Act. |
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa97f/34a064cc/a517c30/70e957f/922917196/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | Friends - I wanted to take a moment to celebrate: Today marks the 53rd anniversary of the passage of the Voting Rights Act. My parents had to fight for the right to vote, and they taught me the importance of casting a ballot. If we want to have a voice in our government - in how our government serves us - voting is the only mechanism that guarantees our voices will be heard. That is a lesson that has stuck with me. **[On today of all days, the anniversary of the Voting Rights Act, sign on to say you commit to voting in this election. It is so important that every Georgian makes their voice heard.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa97f/34a064cc/a517c30/70e957c/922917196/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwODA2MTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/s/commit-to-vote?source=IE080618-full")** When I learned that 800,000 people of color in Georgia were not registered to vote, I launched The New Georgia Project to fix that problem. In just over two years, New Georgia Project submitted 200,000 voter registration applications, fought voter suppression and voter intimidation, and successfully restored more than 30,000 illegally cancelled voters to the rolls. **Today, I am running for governor to build a Georgia that listens to every community. Expanding access to the ballot is a critical component of our vision.** **[Add your name if you're committed to voting in this election. Thank you for your commitment, and thank you for your vote.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa97f/34a064cc/a517c30/70e957c/922917196/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwODA2MTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/s/commit-to-vote?source=IE080618-full")** Thank you for making your voice heard. All my best, Stacey
---

clean_body: Today marks the 53rd anniversary of the passage of the Voting Rights Act. | "" | | | Friends - I wanted to take a moment to celebrate: Today marks the 53rd anniversary of the passage of the Voting Rights Act. My parents had to fight for the right to vote, and they taught me the importance of casting a ballot. If we want to have a voice in our government - in how our government serves us - voting is the only mechanism that guarantees our voices will be heard. That is a lesson that has stuck with me. "" When I learned that 800,000 people of color in Georgia were not registered to vote, I launched The New Georgia Project to fix that problem. In just over two years, New Georgia Project submitted 200,000 voter registration applications, fought voter suppression and voter intimidation, and successfully restored more than 30,000 illegally cancelled voters to the rolls. Today, I am running for governor to build a Georgia that listens to every community. Expanding access to the ballot is a critical component of our vision. "" Thank you for making your voice heard. All my best, Stacey "" | "" "" "" 1270 Caroline Street Suite D120-447 Atlanta, GA 30307 (C)2018 Stacey Abrams for Governor, All rights reserved "" | | Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
